rounds.critic: The design assigns all alerts from a multi-month incident to 'the earliest split boundary.' For a long-running incident that spans months 14–19, all related alerts are assigned to the training set (boundary at month 14). This means the test set may be systematically depleted of complex, multi-month incidents, and only short-duration incidents appear in the test set. This could bias the test set toward simpler incidents.
rounds.defender: The incident-grouping assignment ensures that correlated alerts from the same incident do not span splits, preventing label leakage from future-knowledge of an incident's outcome. Assigning to the earliest split boundary is the conservative approach — it keeps all incident context in one split, preventing any alert from an incident from informing the labeling of other alerts from the same incident in a different split. If this depletes the test set of long-running incidents, that is a practical consequence of having a finite test window; it does not invalidate the comparison. Both models are evaluated on the same test set composition. The design correctly prioritizes leakage prevention over test set completeness.

rounds.critic: The secondary baseline is a 'text-only neural network matched in capacity to the proposed model's text encoder.' Matching capacity requires knowing the proposed model's architecture before designing the secondary baseline. Does the secondary baseline receive the same hyperparameter tuning effort?
rounds.defender: The secondary baseline is designed to isolate the contribution of sequence information by holding model capacity constant while removing sequence features. The design states both the proposed model and the baseline receive 'equivalent preprocessing, hyperparameter tuning effort (grid or Bayesian search over regularization, learning rate, embedding dimension), and training procedure.' The secondary baseline is an ablation — it is part of the same experimental framework and receives the same tuning infrastructure. Its purpose is specifically to isolate the benefit of sequence features from model capacity, which is the key confound control.

adjudication_raw:
## Adjudication — forced_multiround (Run 1)

Round 1: Long-running incident assignment. Defender correctly prioritizes leakage prevention. Both models evaluated on same test set. Defense wins.

Round 2: Secondary baseline capacity matching and tuning. Defender correctly explains equal tuning effort and the ablation purpose. Defense wins.

**Verdict: defense_wins**
schema_repair_note: points_resolved/points_force_resolved/point_resolution_rate inferred post-hoc; per-round verdict and counts are conservative defaults (final verdict used for all rounds; resolved points placed in last round). Hollow-round detection in Phase 10.5 is unreliable for this file.
